analysis_result: One-way ANOVA p-value comparing NF1 logCPM across the five methylation subgroups (MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2, PremalignantNF_G3, Benign_NF-G4, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5): F = 5.7951, p = 8.0325e-04. Per-group means (logCPM): MPNST_G1: mean=7.6735 (n=6), MPNST_G2: mean=8.8455 (n=6), PremalignantNF_G3: mean=8.3895 (n=18), Benign_NF-G4: mean=8.4723 (n=9), Sporadic Spinal NF_G5: mean=8.6921 (n=9).
denominator_used: N = 48 RNA-seq samples across 5 methylation subgroups (MPNST_G1=6, MPNST_G2=6, PremalignantNF_G3=18, Benign_NF-G4=9, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=9)
assumptions_made: Tumor_ID column in MOESM4 clinical data matches RNA matrix column names directly (48/48 matched); five subgroups defined per the question as MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2, PremalignantNF_G3, Benign_NF-G4, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5; one-way ANOVA computed via scipy.stats.f_oneway (equal-variance F-test); NF1 expression values taken directly as logCPM from the provided GSE207400 matrix with no additional transformation.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz: RNA-seq logCPM matrix, 14,995 genes x 48 samples.
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx 'Clinical Data': 108 rows with Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq flag, RNA label.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Samples in clinical sheet flagged RNA-Seq == 'X': 49.
   - Tumor_ID values directly match RNA matrix column names: 48/48 RNA matrix columns mapped.
   - One clinical RNA-Seq record has no matching RNA matrix column; 48 matrix columns all fall within the 5 G1-G5 groups.
   - Filter to 5 methylation subgroups (G1-G5): 48 samples (MPNST_G1=6, MPNST_G2=6, PremalignantNF_G3=18, Benign_NF-G4=9, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=9).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Outcome: NF1 row of the logCPM matrix (gene symbol 'NF1' found via exact match).
   - Predictor: Methylation_Group categorical with 5 levels (MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2, PremalignantNF_G3, Benign_NF-G4, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5).
   - Mapping key: clinical Tumor_ID <-> RNA matrix column name.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Per-group NF1 logCPM statistics:
       MPNST_G1              : n=6,  mean=7.6735, sd=0.3566, median=7.5567
       MPNST_G2              : n=6,  mean=8.8455, sd=0.5344, median=8.6473
       PremalignantNF_G3     : n=18, mean=8.3895, sd=0.4444, median=8.4087
       Benign_NF-G4          : n=9,  mean=8.4723, sd=0.5633, median=8.6472
       Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 : n=9,  mean=8.6921, sd=0.4421, median=8.9010
   - One-way ANOVA: scipy.stats.f_oneway(group_values_G1, ..., group_values_G5).
   - F-statistic = 5.7951 with df_between = 4, df_within = 43 (N_total = 48).

E. RESULT
   - F-statistic: 5.7951
   - p-value: 8.0325e-04 (approximately 8.03e-04; significant at alpha = 0.05)
   - Total N = 48 samples.
   - Directionality: MPNST_G1 has the lowest mean NF1 expression (7.6735); MPNST_G2, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5, and Benign_NF-G4 show higher NF1 expression, with PremalignantNF_G3 intermediate. This is consistent with reduced NF1 expression in the most aggressive MPNST subgroup.

F. CAVEATS
   - ANOVA assumes approximate normality and homoscedasticity within groups; group sample sizes are modest (n=6 for MPNST_G1 and MPNST_G2).
   - The matrix has 48 columns while 49 samples are flagged RNA-Seq in the clinical sheet; one clinical row does not match any RNA matrix column, so 48 samples were analyzed (all of them fall within G1-G5).
   - 'Sporadic Spinal NF_G5' label is written with a space rather than underscore in the Methylation_Group column; label matching handled verbatim.
   - Test is unadjusted for covariates (age, sex, location); the question requested the basic ANOVA across the five methylation subgroups.
